static.description: A minimal Flask REST API that provides personalised greetings, records them in memory, and exposes health-check and listing endpoints.
domain_concepts: Greeting: A recorded personalised message containing a user's name and a hello message, stored in the in-memory list.
Health Check: A lightweight endpoint (/health) that returns an 'ok' status to confirm the service is running.
Service Root: The index endpoint (/) that returns the service name and version metadata.

dynamic.architecture_summary: A single-module Flask application (app.py) that defines four REST endpoints and stores greetings in an in-memory list. There is no database or external dependency beyond Flask itself. Tests live in a dedicated tests/ directory and exercise the endpoints through Flask's test client.

key_components.name: app
key_components.responsibility: Defines the Flask application, all REST endpoints, and the in-memory greetings store.

key_components.name: test_app
key_components.responsibility: Contains tests for the API endpoints.

agent_safe_operations: Adding new GET/POST endpoints in app.py
Adding new test files or test functions in tests/
Updating greeting logic or response format
Adding new utility modules alongside app.py
agent_forbidden_operations: Removing or renaming existing public endpoints (/, /health, /greet/<name>, /greetings) without updating tests
Changing the Flask app variable name 'app' which tests likely import directly
Introducing database dependencies without corresponding migration/setup instructions
potential_pitfalls: The _greetings list is module-level state — it persists across requests within a process but resets on restart; tests that don't reset the list between runs may see stale data.
Flask's test client must import 'app' from app.py; renaming that symbol breaks test discovery.
All endpoints are GET-only; adding POST/PUT endpoints requires careful attention since the existing pattern uses @app.get() shorthand.
No CORS or authentication is configured; adding middleware may change response headers that tests assert on.
The in-memory store is a plain list with no thread safety; concurrent requests under a threaded server could cause race conditions.
